title: Shared Object Views on Dictionary and Migration Tabs
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to use the same saved Object Views on the Dictionary and Migration tabs that I use on the Entity Diagram, so that I can focus on different subsets of my Salesforce objects across all views.
description:
The Object Views feature (saved view configurations that show/hide objects) is extended from the Entity Diagram to the Object Dictionary scorecard page. The same ERD-style side panel with checkboxes for object selection is added to the Dictionary and Migration tabs.

When a view is selected, objects not included in the view are hidden from the table and the breadcrumb dropdown. Views are shared across ERD, Dictionary, and Migration — the same saved views appear in all three. However, the selected view does not persist when navigating between tabs (not sticky across Dictionary and ERD).

The view dropdown width is constrained to match the ERD dropdown for visual consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Object Views dropdown appears on the Dictionary scorecard page
Object Views dropdown appears on the Migration scorecard page
Selecting a view hides non-included objects from the table and the dropdown
Deselecting a view hides it from the table and the object dropdown
Same saved views are available across ERD, Dictionary, and Migration
View selection does not persist when navigating between Dictionary and ERD
View dropdown width matches the ERD dropdown styling
ux_changes: Adds the Object Views dropdown and side panel to the Dictionary and Migration scorecard pages. Toggling views shows/hides objects in the table and breadcrumb dropdown. Dropdown width is constrained for visual consistency with ERD.
middleware_changes: Reuses existing view data and logic from the ERD feature. View filtering is applied to the scorecard object list on the server side or via client-side filtering.
implementation_notes: Views are the same data entities used by the ERD feature — no separate view storage for Dictionary vs ERD. The dropdown width was adjusted after initial review to match the ERD dropdown.
comment_summary: Developer asked whether views should be standalone or shared with ERD. Product owner clarified they should reuse the same ERD view data and apply to both Dictionary and Migration tabs. After staging review, the dropdown width was refined for consistency.
